title: Kore - An easy to use web platform for C and Python

quote.text: The difference between theory and practice is that in theory, there is no difference between theory and practice
quote.author: Richard Moore

links.title: Kore - An easy to use web platform for C and Python
links.description:
Kore is an easy to use web platform for writing scalable, concurrent APIs in C or Python.
Its primary goals are security, scalability and allowing rapid development and deployment of such APIs.
Because of this Kore is an ideal candidate for building robust, scalable and secure web things.

links.title: Uncommon Use Cases For CSS Pseudo Elements
links.description: Pseudo-elements are in use for a long time. However, there are some uses cases that are not entirely known across developers.

links.title: Getting Started with GraphQL
links.description: Since open-sourcing GraphQL specification in 2015, it gained a lot of popularity and is now used by many development teams, including giants like GitHub, Twitter or Airbnb. Why so? And what exactly is a GraphQL query? Let's take a look…

links.title: Multi-Value All The Wasm
links.description: Multi-value is a proposed extension to core WebAssembly that enables functions to return many values, among other things. It is also a prerequisite for Wasm interface types.

links.title: Fabric.js Tutorial: Objects and Complex Shapes
links.description: Fabric.js is a drawing library for JavaScript built on top of canvas element. This Fabric.js tutorial shows you how to draw shapes and scale objects.

links.title: Thoughts on Error Handling in Rust
links.description: An opinionated article on the current state of error handling and error handling crates in the Rust language and the Rust community. Absolutely interesting if you are currently learning Rust.

links.title: Compile-time Immutability in TypeScript
links.description: James Wright explores the possibilities for using constructs to achieve compile-time immutability in TypeScript with const assertions and recursive types.

book.title: Elixir in Action
book.author: Saša Jurić
book.description: Elixir in Action teaches you to apply the new Elixir programming language to practical problems associated with scalability, concurrency, fault tolerance, and high availability. Elixir is a modern programming language that takes advantage of BEAM, the Erlang virtual machine, without the burden of Erlang's complex syntax and conventions. Elixir gives you Ruby-like elegance with the power to develop bulletproof distributed server systems that can handle massive numbers of simultaneous clients and run with almost no downtime.
